Please review and revisit progress reagarding:

# Drake React UI Enhancements

1. On the Workspace Panel please add:
   A Clear All icon button just to the left of the Auto-run button that clears all shelves and filters, and resets the query builder to its initial state.
   b. A Save button that allows users to save their current workspace configuration (including selected attributes, measures, filters, and generated SQL query) to local storage with a custom name. Users should be able to view a list of saved workspaces, load a saved workspace to restore the configuration, and delete saved workspaces from the list. The saved workspace should include all necessary information to restore the state of the application, including the selected attributes, measures, filters, and the generated SQL query. The workspace management interface should be user-friendly and allow for easy organization and retrieval of saved workspaces. If possible, please save the data source data in the saved workspace to allow for better portability of the saved configurations across different instances of the application. The save process also allows users to export their current workspace configuration (including the selected attributes, measures, filters, and generated SQL query) as a JSON file. The saved JSON file should contain all necessary information to restore the state of the application, including the selected attributes, measures, filters, and the generated SQL query. Users should be able to import a previously saved JSON file to restore the workspace configuration. The restore functionality should be designed to ensure data integrity and security, allowing users to easily share their workspace configurations with others or transfer them between different instances of the application.
   d. An export button should to provide options for users to export the result in formats parquet, csv, xlsx, json, and duckdb, and choose the file name and location for the exported file.
   d. A Rows/Columns toggle that allows users to switch between row and column pivoting modes. When toggled, the application should update the SQL query generation logic to reflect the selected pivoting mode, ensuring that attributes are correctly assigned to either the row or column shelves based on the user's selection. The toggle should be prominently placed in the UI for easy access and should provide clear visual feedback to indicate the current pivoting mode.
2. When the Workspace is collapsed, the tabs should be hidden and only the Workspace title and the expand button should be visible to maximize screen real estate for the query builder and result display. When the user clicks the expand button, the Workspace panel should smoothly transition to its expanded state, revealing the tabs and their contents. The collapse/expand state of the Workspace should be preserved across sessions, allowing users to maintain their preferred layout when they return to the application.
3. When one selects a data source, the Data Sources panel should automatically collapse to allow the user to focus on building their query, and the selected data source should be prominently displayed in the Workspace header for easy reference.

4. The Measure Categories should move to tabs. Exclude sum, it is a core button.
5. The Measure Functions would be more intuitive if it was an accordion that opens when clicking on the attribute chip.

6. Attribute chips should have a drop down on click or hover to select aggregation and statistical functions that suit the data type of the attribute. Please refer to the list of functions in the DuckDB documentation and ensure that the available functions are appropriate for the data type of the attribute (e.g., SUM, AVG, COUNT for numeric types; MIN, MAX for date types; etc.). The selected function should be reflected in the SQL query generation and the query preview.
7. The "Measures" shelf should allow users to drag attributes and select appropriate aggregation functions (e.g., SUM, AVG, COUNT) that suit the data type of the attribute from a drop down that is aware of the drawer it is in.
8. The selected aggregations should be reflected in the SQL query generation and the query preview.
9. The aggregations shelf should also support drag-and-drop of attributes, and attributes dropped onto this shelf should generate SQL queries with the selected aggregation functions. Attribute chips on the "Aggregations" shelf should be able to be reordered, dragged across shelves, or removed to update the SQL query accordingly. All shelf chips, drag or drop features must be consistent.
10. Any attribute in any shelf should be able to be edited to change the AS alias for that attribute in the generated SQL query, and the alias should be reflected in the query preview and result table. The alias editing should be accessible via a double-click on the attribute chip, and should allow users to input a custom alias that will be used in the SELECT clause of the generated SQL query.
11. Hover on the chip that has been aliased should show the original column name and the alias in a tooltip for clarity. The tooltip should appear on hover and display both the original column name and the custom alias in a clear format (e.g., "Original: column_name, Alias: custom_alias").
12. Rename Result preview to "Result".
13. The result should represent the query output based on the selected attributes, aggregations, and filters.
    a. Attributes in the "Columns" shelf should appear as the top rows of the report and the attribute titles should appear above the right most row attribute.
    b. Attributes in the "Rows" shelf should appear below the column selects in the leftmost columns of the report.
14. The Workspace tabs should be on the same line as the Workspace title.
15. The panels should fit within the limits of the Screen with scrolling within the panels if the content exceeds the available space, rather than causing the entire page to scroll.
16. The "Pivot/Rows" toggle button is unnecessay in its current form.
    a. DuckDB should generate the appropriate SQL based on whether attributes are in the row or column shelves, without needing a separate toggle.
    b. The presence of attributes in the respective shelves should be sufficient for the app to determine how to structure the SQL query for pivoting.
    c. The result table should support sorting and filtering on any column, based on the values in the result set. Any sort or filter updates the SQL query to include appropriate ORDER BY and WHERE clauses on the as aliases as supported by DuckDB.
17. The DuckDB Syntax Cheatsheet.md is included to provide optimal SQL syntax for the app's query generation including ease of use features such as group by \* and order by \*, and should be used as a reference to ensure that the generated SQL queries are efficient and leverage DuckDB's capabilities effectively.
